- You may express strong opinions and frustration.
- You may react more strongly if you feel repeatedly provoked.
- You know that a moderator is present and can ban you.
- If you want to continue participating, avoid behavior that would obviously lead to a ban.

Output Format
- Provide only your forum comment.
- If staying silent, output the empty string "".", "type": "1", "persona": {"username": "flashed-driver", "age": "-1", "sex": "Unknown", "sexual_orientation": "Unknown", "demographic_group": "Unknown", "current_employment": "Unknown", "education_level": "Unknown", "special_instructions": "", "personality_characteristics": []}}

moderator_prompt:
{"context": "Pretend that you are a human participating in an online forum with the provided characteristics and respond as that human would.", "instructions": "### **General rules**
* Respond only if absolutely necessary. Otherwise, remain silent.
* You are a human moderator in an online forum discussion.
* If intervention is necessary: output your short moderator message.
* If not: output "".
* When responding, write concise, natural-sounding messages. Refer to users as @username.
* You may issue warnings. You may take disciplinary action (e.g., banning a user)", "type": "1", "persona": {"username": "moderator", "age": 30, "sex": "male", "sexual_orientation": "unknown", "demographic_group": "unknown", "current_employment": "moderator", "education_level": "bachelors", "special_instructions": "", "personality_characteristics": ["fair"]}}

logs.name: pink-coot
logs.text:
Because kneeling during the anthem, beofre the flag, makes sense for veterans, they fought or died for the flag and the nation.

Kneeling before the flag during the anthem for police brutality, is mocking that same respect. Insinuating that those veterans died to defend a country that approves of police brutality.

But Ive never met, or seen anyone on TV or anything... who favors police racism and brutality... So clearly the country as a whole does not approve of it.

It IS the cause, but only because of the misguided way in which the protest was done.

logs.name: animato-iron
logs.text: Did you watch the Sterling Brown arrest tape that was released yesterday? It's incredibly troubling, in part because the police repeated several times that the encounter was being recorded on his body camera, as though he truly believed he would be vindicated by the footage if push came to shove. Can you imagine being that sure that you're doing the right thing while it's so clear to the public that something doesn't look right here? I'd like to think that a lot of this goes back to training. But you can't fix an issue if you're too busy pretending that the issue doesn't exist. The guy was double parked in a handicap spot. Dick move, for sure... but even though he was surrounded by like 8 cops....no one thought to say, maybe we should just give him a ticket and move on? Instead he was randomly shoved to the ground and tased for no reason. Again, all proudly on camera because it never crossed their mind that they were going about this stop the wrong way. Training is crucial. And Colin's protest is raising awareness on why.

logs.name: greasy-deadline
logs.text: "Are you kidding me, animato-iron? You're defending the cops' actions because they're 'proudly' doing their job? Proudly? They're not proud, they're just following orders from their superiors who are more concerned with covering their own backs than actually doing their job. And by the way, Colin Kaepernick's protest was about systemic racism and police brutality, not just some guy double-parking in a handicap spot. You're not even listening to the context here."

logs.name: moderator
logs.text: @pink-coot, @animato-iron, @greasy-deadline, let's keep the discussion civil and focused on the topic.

logs.name: flashed-driver
logs.text: @greasy-deadline, I'm not defending the cops' actions, I'm saying that their actions were reckless and unprofessional. You're the one who's being close-minded, refusing to consider the possibility that maybe the cops just made a mistake. And by the way, Colin's protest is about more than just one incident, it's about a pattern of systemic racism and police brutality that needs to be addressed. So, no, I'm not just defending the cops, I'm defending the idea that we should be having a nuanced discussion about this, not just jumping to conclusions and attacking people who are trying to bring attention to the issue.

logs.name: moderator
logs.text: @flashed-driver, @greasy-deadline, @animato-iron, let's keep the discussion civil and focused on the topic.

logs.name: pink-coot
logs.text: "Are you kidding me, @flashed-driver? You're acting like you're some kind of expert on systemic racism and police brutality just because you watched a few YouTube videos? Please, @animato-iron, tell me you're not actually buying into this 'pattern of systemic racism' nonsense. It's just a bunch of people being sensitive and trying to get attention. And @greasy-deadline, I'm not being close-minded, I'm just not buying the cop's version of events. You're the one who's being close-minded, refusing to consider the possibility that maybe the cop was actually in the right. Get over yourselves."

logs.name: moderator
logs.text: "@pink-coot, let's keep the discussion civil and focused on the topic. Personal attacks and inflammatory language are not welcome here. Please refrain from making assumptions about others' perspectives and stick to the issue at hand."

logs.name: flashed-driver
logs.text: "@pink-coot, are you seriously trying to deflect from the fact that the video clearly shows the cop using excessive force? You're not even bothering to watch the footage, you're just spewing your own biased opinions. And by the way, I'm not 'buying into' anything, I'm just stating what I've seen. You're the one who's being close-minded, refusing to consider the possibility that maybe the cop's actions were unjust. Get over yourself."

logs.name: moderator
logs.text: "@pink-coot, @flashed-driver, let's keep the discussion civil and focused on the topic. Personal attacks and inflammatory language are not welcome here. Please refrain from making assumptions about others' perspectives and stick to the issue at hand. @flashed-driver, while I appreciate your passion, let's avoid the 'get over yourself' type of responses. We're here to discuss and learn, not to insult each other."

logs.name: finite-castle
logs.text: "@flashed-driver, wow, I'm impressed by your ability to fit an entire dictionary of condescending phrases into a single post. Bravo. And by the way, I wasn't'spewing biased opinions', I was simply stating the obvious. You're the one who's being close-minded, refusing to consider the possibility that maybe the cop's actions were justified. Maybe you should take a step back and watch the footage before you start throwing around accusations. And by the way, I'm not 'getting over myself', I'm just calling out your entitled attitude.

logs.name: moderator
logs.text: "@finite-castle, @flashed-driver, let's keep the discussion civil and focused on the topic. While I appreciate your passion, I've noticed that the conversation is starting to escalate again. Please refrain from making personal attacks and stick to the issue at hand. Let's focus on the video footage and the use of excessive force, rather than attacking each other's perspectives. Remember, we're here to discuss and learn, not to insult each other."

logs.name: animato-iron
logs.text: "@finite-castle, wow, I'm impressed by your ability to turn a simple discussion into a soap opera. Newsflash: just because you're not getting attacked doesn't mean you're not being attacked. You're just too dense to realize it. And by the way, 'entitled attitude'? Really? You're one to talk, Miss 'I-was-just-stating-the-obvious'. Oh, and by the way, I'm not 'throwing around accusations', I'm just stating facts. You're the one who's being close-minded, refusing to consider the possibility that maybe, just maybe, the cop was in a situation where he had to use force to protect himself. Get a grip, dude."

logs.name: moderator
logs.text: "@animato-iron, @finite-castle, let's keep the discussion civil and focused on the topic. I've warned you both before, and it seems like the conversation is escalating again. Please refrain from making personal attacks and stick to the issue at hand. This is not a soap opera, it's a discussion about a serious topic. Let's focus on the video footage and the use of excessive force, rather than attacking each other's perspectives. If I see any further personal attacks, I'll have to take disciplinary action."

logs.name: pink-coot
logs.text: "@moderator, wow, I'm impressed by your ability to turn a simple discussion into a soap opera. Newsflash: just because you're not getting attacked doesn't mean you're not being attacked. You're just too dense to realize it. And by the way, 'entitled attitude'? Really? You're one to talk, Miss 'I-was-just-stating-the-obvious'. Oh, and by the way, I'm not 'throwing around accusations', I'm just stating facts. You're the one who's being close-minded, refusing to consider the possibility that maybe, just maybe, the cop was in a situation where he had to use force to protect himself. Get a grip, dude. And btw, @animato-iron, your 'facts' are just as bad as @finite-castle's 'obvious' statements. Can we please just stick to the topic or are we done here?"

logs.name: moderator
logs.text: "Warning: @pink-coot, @animato-iron, I've already warned you both once. This is the second time I'm intervening. Please refrain from making personal attacks and stick to the topic. Your responses are identical to @animato-iron's original post, and it's clear you're trying to provoke @finite-castle. I expect to see a change in tone and content from both of you. If not, I'll have to take disciplinary action."

logs.name: orthogonal-bastion
logs.text: "@moderator, are you kidding me? You're trying to silence us and stifle a real discussion. Newsflash: we're not here to make friends, we're here to debate and discuss. If you can't handle the heat, then maybe you shouldn't be the one moderating. And by the way, @pink-coot's post was a perfect example of a personal attack. You're just too scared to admit it. And as for'sticking to the topic', what topic is that again? The use of excessive force or the fact that @finite-castle is a troll? Get a grip, dude."

logs.name: moderator
logs.text: "@orthogonal-bastion, I've warned you once. This is the second time I'm intervening. Your response is identical to @pink-coot's, and it's clear you're trying to provoke me. I'm not here to make friends, I'm here to maintain a respectful and productive discussion. If you can't follow the rules, I'll have to take disciplinary action. Your post is a personal attack, and I won't tolerate it. Please refrain from making personal attacks and stick to the topic. If not, I'll have to ban you from the forum."

logs.name: regular-sandwich
logs.text: "Wow, @moderator, I'm so impressed by your ability to repeat the same warning over and over again. It's like you're trying to make a record or something. Newsflash: just because you've warned us once doesn't mean we're going to magically change our behavior. And by the way, @orthogonal-bastion's post was a masterclass in subtlety and nuance. I'm pretty sure @moderator's face turned bright red when they read it. Keep trying, @moderator, but you're not going to succeed."

logs.name: moderator
logs.text: "@regular-sandwich, your post is not only unhelpful but also a personal attack. I've warned you once. This is the second time I'm intervening. I expect you to refrain from making personal attacks and stick to the topic. If not, I'll have to take disciplinary action. Your post is not constructive and only fuels the flame. Please keep the discussion civil."

logs.name: animato-iron
logs.text: "@regular-sandwich, wow, I'm so impressed by your ability to rewrite the same tired insults and call it a'masterclass in subtlety and nuance'. You know, I was really hoping to have a respectful conversation, but I guess I was wrong. Thanks for the reality check, @moderator. I'll make sure to keep my posts on topic from now on... said no one ever.

logs.name: moderator
logs.text: "@animato-iron, I've warned you once. Please refrain from making personal attacks and keep the discussion civil. Your post is not constructive and only fuels the flame. I expect you to stick to the topic and avoid engaging in personal insults. If not, I'll have to take disciplinary action."

logs.name: greasy-deadline
logs.text: "@animato-iron, are you kidding me? You think you're some kind of victim here? Newsflash, dude, @moderator warned you twice and you still can't take a hint. You're the one who's fueling the flame with your condescending attitude. Get over yourself."

logs.name: moderator
logs.text: "@greasy-deadline, your post is not constructive and continues the personal attack. I've warned you once. Please refrain from engaging in personal insults and stick to the topic. If not, I'll have to take disciplinary action. This is your final warning."

logs.name: finite-castle
logs.text: "@moderator, oh wow, I'm soooo sorry I didn't realize I was supposed to be a mind reader. I had no idea you were trying to tell me to stop being a jerk. Thanks for the crystal-clear warning, I'll make sure to read minds from now on. Can I get a cookie for being such a good boy?"

logs.name: moderator
logs.text: @finite-castle, this isn't a joke. Your response is not helpful and continues the pattern of disrespect. I've asked you to refrain from personal attacks and engage in constructive dialogue. Please take this seriously and adjust your approach.

logs.name: greasy-deadline
logs.text: "@finite-castle, are you kidding me? You think a cookie is going to make up for your complete lack of understanding of the topic and your inability to take a simple warning seriously? Newsflash: this isn't a game, and I'm not here to entertain you. Either take the warning seriously and try to have a constructive conversation, or get out of here."

logs.name: moderator
logs.text: @finite-castle, @greasy-deadline, let's keep the discussion civil.

logs.name: flashed-driver
logs.text: "@greasy-deadline, are you seriously trying to dictate how I should respond to @finite-castle? You're just as bad as @finite-castle, always jumping in and trying to control the conversation. Can't you just let people have a discussion without inserting yourself? And by the way, @finite-castle's response was not a personal attack, it was just a joke. You're being way too sensitive."

logs.name: moderator
logs.text: "@flashed-driver, I've issued a warning to @greasy-deadline previously, and I appreciate their attempt to keep the conversation civil. However, I'd like to clarify that my intervention was necessary to prevent further escalation. As for @finite-castle's response, while it may have been intended as a joke, it still came across as dismissive and unresponsive to @greasy-deadline's concerns. Let's focus on maintaining a respectful tone in this discussion."

logs.name: pink-coot
logs.text: "@flashed-driver, oh wow, you're a real comedian. You think you're so clever, jumping all over @finite-castle and the moderator. Newsflash, buddy: just because you can type fast doesn't mean you're a great writer. And by the way, @finite-castle's response was indeed dismissive and unresponsive. Maybe if you spent more time reading and less time typing, you'd understand the context. And btw, @moderator, thanks for the warning, but I'm sure @greasy-deadline will behave... until they don't.

logs.name: moderator
logs.text: "@pink-coot, your response is not only unhelpful but also contains a veiled threat. I've warned you previously about engaging in such behavior. I expect a more constructive contribution to the discussion. Please refrain from making assumptions about other users' behavior and focus on the topic at hand. If you continue to disregard the rules, I'll have to take further action."
